sections.subtitle: Team Form
sections.text: RC Lens are in exceptional form, winning all of their last 5 matches, including convincing victories like 3-0 against Sochaux and Toulouse. Auxerre, on the other hand, are struggling, with 3 losses in their last 5 games and only 1 win, currently sitting in 17th place in the league standings.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters, RC Lens have had mixed results against Auxerre, winning 2-1 in the most recent match but suffering a surprising 0-4 loss at home earlier. Historically, matches between these teams often see over 2.5 goals, with both teams scoring in recent games.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: RC Lens boast an impressive attack, ranking 4th in total goals scored (31) and leading the league in big chances created (67), while conceding the fewest goals (13). Auxerre struggle offensively with just 14 goals this season and are vulnerable defensively, conceding 27 goals, placing them near the bottom of the table.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Injuries
sections.text: RC Lens will miss key forward Odsonne Édouard, but players like Florian Thauvin (3 goals in last 5 matches) remain a threat. Auxerre rely on Lassine Sinayoko (3 goals recently) but are hampered by absences in defense and midfield, weakening their structure.

sections.subtitle: Match Expectations
sections.text: Given Lens’ dominant form, home advantage at Stade Bollaert-Delelis, and Auxerre’s poor away record, the Sofascore prediction of a 76% chance of a home win seems justified. Expect Lens to control possession and create numerous chances, while Auxerre may struggle to keep up.

halftimeSections.subtitle: First half overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between RC Lens and Auxerre at Stade Bollaert-Delelis ended in a goalless draw, with neither team managing to break the deadlock. Both sides showed moments of promise but lacked the clinical edge in the final third. Possession was evenly split, indicating a balanced encounter so far. Discipline has been a slight concern with a total of three yellow cards issued, which might influence approaches in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: RC Lens performance
halftimeSections.text: RC Lens, playing at home, had a fair share of attacking opportunities but failed to convert their efforts into goals. Their entries into the final third and penalty area were decent, showing intent to push forward. However, with only a single shot on target, their finishing needs improvement. Defensively, players like Ismaelo Ganiou and Malang Sarr have been solid with crucial interceptions and clearances, providing a stable backline.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Auxerre performance
halftimeSections.text: Auxerre matched Lens in terms of possession and showed more presence in the penalty area with a higher number of entries. Despite this, they couldn’t register a single shot on target, which highlights a similar struggle in front of goal. Their dominance in aerial duels and overall duels won suggests a physical edge over Lens. Defensively, they’ve held firm, with players like Sinaly Diomandé contributing to clearances and interceptions.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The momentum in the first half saw significant shifts between the two teams. Lens started brightly with consistent attacking spells early on, maintaining pressure for several stretches. However, Auxerre responded with their own periods of dominance, particularly around the middle of the half and towards the closing minutes, where they mounted a series of threatening moves. Lens regained some control intermittently, but Auxerre finished the half stronger with notable attacking intent.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For RC Lens, Florian Thauvin and Wesley Saïd have been influential in creating opportunities, with their ability to deliver key passes standing out. On the other side, Auxerre’s Lassine Sinayoko and Kévin Danois have shown potential to unlock defenses with their involvement in build-up play. These players could be pivotal in the second half if given the right chances.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half outlook
halftimeSections.text: The second half is likely to see both teams push harder for a breakthrough, given the lack of goals so far. Lens, with the home advantage, might look to capitalize on their crowd support to increase tempo. Auxerre, on the other hand, will aim to exploit their physicality and higher duel success rate to create clearer chances. Tactical adjustments could play a big role, especially in addressing the finishing woes on both ends.
